rationale: The main connective (def:logical-connective) of a molecular formula (def:molecular-formula) is 'the connective introduced at the final formation step'. That this final step is UNIQUE -- hence the main connective is well-defined -- is exactly unique readability (thm:unique-decomposition), which was missing. This is the same well-definedness dependency that parse-tree (0116) and subformula (0120) already carry.
licensing_quote: the connective introduced at the final formation step
notes: Kept the existing three deps; added thm:unique-decomposition as the well-definedness justification (Rule 4). def:well-formed-formula is the substrate (the phi, psi in neg-phi and (phi-circ-psi)); def:molecular-formula scopes the notion (atomic formulas have no main connective).
